site.title: Gateway Experience
site.tagline: Hemi-Sync learning path — 6 waves, guided sessions, progress journal.

home.title: Gateway Experience
home.subtitle: Explore all six Gateway waves with stereo playback, L/R/Beat frequency display, and local progress tracking.
home.editionsIntro: Classic release (FLAC, 6 waves) or Gateway Experience v2 (English MP3, 7 waves including Prospecting and Odyssey).

start.title: Getting started
start.intro: New to the Gateway Experience? Follow these steps and read the suggested documents before asking the same questions over and over — you’ll find answers to most of them there.
start.tipsTitle: Quick tips — things not to do
start.tipsIntro: Practical community advice so early sessions aren’t derailed by common mistakes.
start.faqIntro: Links to the most helpful threads (r/gatewaytapes wiki).
start.source: Guide adapted from the r/Sbreggo / r/gatewaytapes community — external resources are not affiliated with the Monroe Institute.

waves.title: The six waves
waves.intro: Each wave introduces a Focus level and progressive exercises. Waves I–II include French audio; waves III–VI are English FLAC only.
waves.v2Intro: Seven English MP3 waves — adds Prospecting (VI) and Odyssey (VII) beyond the classic six-wave path.

session.playerHint: No signal processing — L/R spectrum (20 Hz–4 kHz, log scale) and documented frequency metadata.
session.transcriptPlaceholder: Pedagogical script will be extracted from scanned manuals (OCR). Until then, follow the wave PDF manual.
session.journalPlaceholder: Personal notes (stored locally in your browser).
session.instructionsLead: Notes from the wave manual — what this exercise asks you to do.
session.listenLead: Use headphones in a quiet space. Follow the voice on the track; glance back at the instructions if needed.
session.reflectLead: Capture impressions while they are fresh, then mark the session complete.
session.prepTips: Quiet space · Comfortable posture · Allow 45–60 min · Do not drive while listening

player.beatTargetNote: Binaural beat target (|L−R|) — shown here, not as a spectrum peak
player.detectHint: Live FFT: strongest tone per ear (80–1200 Hz). Estimated binaural beat ≈ |L − R|. Voice and music can dominate over Hemi-Sync carriers.
player.bandDistHint: Share of stereo audio energy in each EEG band — from the FFT, not brain electrodes. Hemi-Sync beats often show in θ/α.
player.ciaReportTitle: Why L / R / Beat? — CIA Gateway report (1983)
player.ciaReportBody: The Analysis and Assessment of Gateway Process report (Lt. Col. Wayne M. McDonnell, US Army, 1983) explains Hemi-Sync: slightly different frequencies in each ear produce a binaural beat (|L − R|). That beat is intended to entrain brainwave activity toward Focus states (10, 12, 15, 21…).
player.ciaReportSpectrum: The two spectra below show per-channel energy from 20 Hz to 4 kHz (log scale) — Hemi-Sync carriers, harmonics, and guided voice. The purple line marks a documented carrier frequency when known.
player.noFlac: Your browser may not play FLAC. Download the file for local playback.
player.pagesNoMediaTitle: Audio not available on this online site
player.pagesNoMediaBody: No media source configured for this build. Locally: clone with data/ and npm run dev. On GitHub Pages, files must be pushed to LFS on main.
player.loadError: Could not load audio (file missing or unsupported format).

supplements.title: Supplements
supplements.intro: Mind Food and Humain Plus — complementary tracks from the French corpus.

glossary.title: Focus glossary
glossary.intro: Numbered consciousness states used in the Gateway Experience — definitions summarized from the 1983 CIA Gateway report (McDonnell).

progress.title: My progress
progress.intro: Local tracking (localStorage) — no data is sent to a server.

footer.tagline: Personal educational use — Hemi-Sync / Monroe Institute.
